archimate:DataObject.name: W3C Shapes Constraint Language
archimate:DataObject.documentation:
Description: The Shapes Constraint Language (SHACL) is a language and framework for describing and validating the structure and constraints of RDF (Resource Description Framework) graphs. RDF is a standard for representing data in a graph format, widely used in the Semantic Web.
Interoperability Dimension: Structural IoP
Publisher: W3C
Source: https://www.w3.org/TR/shacl/
LOST view: SV-Agnostic Ontologies
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/W3CShapesConstraintLanguageDataObject
EIRA concept: eira:SolutionBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/W3CShapesConstraintLanguageDataObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Realises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/Ontology

properties.value: The Shapes Constraint Language (SHACL) is a language and framework for describing and validating the structure and constraints of RDF (Resource Description Framework) graphs. RDF is a standard for representing data in a graph format, widely used in the Semantic Web.
